target.name: Pickle Robot Company

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $113M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Pickle Robot Company is a Series B robotics and manufacturing venture that has raised $113M to date. The comparable universe consists of nine private robotics firms at the same stage, anchored by companies including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), and Dexterity ($1.6B), alongside one public comparable.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $113M total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $215M. The peer-median method examines six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.1B, though the range is wide: $300M to $10.0B. This dispersion reflects substantial heterogeneity in robotics company valuations at this stage.

Triangulation of both methods produces a valuation range of $182M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median estimate of $645M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower end, while peer multiples support the higher end. Key uncertainties include Pickle's revenue traction and customer concentration relative to the peer set—particularly the outlier valuations of 1X Technologies and smaller players like Fairmat. The wide peer range suggests that unobserved differences in market position, technology differentiation, or fundraising momentum materially affect valuation outcomes in this sector.
